observations.summary: The description of the colossal stone head emerging from the waves with barnacles like jewels of corruption parallels the motif of ancient, decayed stone and corruption found in the narrative context, emphasizing themes of ancient decay and profound coldness.

source_evidence.quote: The face was carved from drowned stone—ancient, impassive, radiating an inhuman profound cold. Eyes wide as abysses, blacker than the deepest trench in the sea floor. Seaweed, thick and rotting, draped the chin like a hideous beard. Barnacles, sharp and encrusted, clung to the cheeks like jewels of corruption.

target_evidence.quote: The suffocating emptiness smelled of sulfur and expectant soil, but h middle=then beneath his clothing, whispering, "Mine," hoarding his own delusion. He gathered the ever-burgeoning accumulation until his very form was distended, crushed. He became a lonesome pillar of stone in a barren field, the mass of the precious metal squeezing the breath from his lungs.
